visa_types.name: Child Passport
visa_types.documents_required:
It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document which is certified and a photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator original document (foreign birth) must be legalised/apostilled.
- Form BI-73 - Passport Application Form
Applicant to complete the section A and sign in section E of the DHA 73 form.
Section B of this form will complete upon your appointment at office.
Parental consent on page 2 section C of DHA 73 must be signed in the office if applicant is under 18 years and both parents must be present at the office for the passport of applicants below 18 years.
If one parent is not in The Netherlands, consent must be obtained from the Home Affairs in South Africa or from a local South African Embassy to complete the DHA-73 form and give consent for the child’s passport application where the other parent is residing.
In a case where parents have passed on, the lawful guardian will submit the certified copy of the court order certificate of adoption and certified copy of the first page of his/her identity document and passport.
- Form BI-529 - Determination of Citizenship Status
This form is required for all passport applications made outside of South Africa.
Please mark N/A to questions that are not applicable. Do not leave anything blank. Please note questions A9 & A10 MUST be answered and section 'E' signed by the parent of the child or the form will be returned.
- Form DI-47 Loss of passport report and police report
To be submitted for lost/stolen passport with police report - if applicable.
- Bring the original and copies of your below documents
This Application Centre can certify the copy if the original is shown.
- South Africa current passport.
- South Africa birth certificate.
- South Africa ID books / smart ID cards of both parents.
- South Africa passports of both parents.
- A certified copy of your foreign citizenship certificate and your dual citizenship certificate - if applicable
If you have dual nationality, please provide proof of how and when you acquired the foreign nationality, such Naturalization and retention or Registration Certificate.
FOREIGN PASSPORT OR ID IS NOT PROOF OF HOW AND WHEN YOU ACQUIRED FOREIGN CITIZENSHIP.
In cases of the applicant who acquired foreign citizenship through descent from a parent, a certified copy of your own full birth certificate showing the birth places of your parent(s) is required. Under circumstances where even the parents acquired such citizenship through descent, a letter from the relevant authorities of the country concerned will suffice.
If you are a naturalised South African, a certified copy of your Naturalisation Certificate.
Documents issued in a foreign language (if not in English) must be officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
This Application Centre can certify the copy as a “true copy of the original” if the original is provided.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Once you have made your application, you will have to pay the fee at the Application Centre by Credit/Debit card only. Kindly note that other modes of payment are not accepted.
VFS Global charges a service fee – 60 USD Euros on top of the below consular fees and additional services fees (https://services.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/zap/additional-services).

[zap > moz > en > Consular Service > Passport > Fees > table]
visa_types.processing_time:
The standard processing time is approximately 6 to 8 weeks from the date of submission of the application.
Please note that, following submission, applications are subject to additional verification and screening by the relevant authorities.
Applicants may be requested to:
- Provide additional supporting documentations;
- Re-submit biometric data (e.g. where fingerprint or photograph quality does not meet required standards);
- Re-submit application forms (in instances of incomplete or inaccurate information in currently submitted forms).
Where additional requirements arise, processing times may be extended accordingly.
Applicants are strongly advised to schedule an appointment at least eighteen (18) months prior to the expiry date of their current passport in order to ensure the timely receipt of travel documents and to avoid potential delays.
Applicants are further advised not to make any confirmed or non-refundable travel arrangements until the new passport has been issued and successfully received.
Disclaimer
Please be informed that VFS Global does not have any influence over the processing timelines for passport applications, first identity document applications, or birth registration services.
visa_types.forms:
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “N/A” (not applicable).
- DHA - 73_Passport Application (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ChDh49d3AmEsE5y0gKvul/456c6ff0f95ed0cda72b8e5d12e12a19/dha-73.pdf)
- BI-529_Determination of Citizenship (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3MDVNjoL4DEar34u8rdR1s/86b75f81835fff7663112fe88d30bd22/BI-529-determination-of-citizenship.pdf)
- DI – 47_Loss Report (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/22y5oLag2SNxtzNnoNDpdP/0a07f2396b45c18da511cd5aeae3990d/DI-47_Loss_Report.pdf)
- Registration of divorce (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4wHrprxkynDG7cbMqo4vSQ/1a64acaeee5eb005b1900e716cfd5733/REGISTRATION_OF_DIVORCE.pdf)
- Registration of Marriage (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/56n8Be3ZSuYD4lI1wky69t/d58b2281e14bd0f21c6dba320da947fc/Registration_of_Marriage.pdf)
- Namibian Questionnaire (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1qNJWtnX7M22XrWBehlLEf/94d933661286544292727681b7a02805/NAMIBIAN_QUESTIONNAIRE.pdf)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK in BLOCK LETTERS only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “N/A” (not applicable).
It takes six to eight weeks to process new South African documents. To ensure that applicants receive their documents on time, they are advised to please book an appointment at least EIGHTEEN months before expiry of current passport to avoid any delays. Please do not make firm travel arrangements before obtaining your passport.
Kindly note that both parents and the child must be present at the Application Center for the child passport application.
If applicant is under 15 and a half years, they are not required to provide fingerprints.
If the child is older than 15 and a half years or turning 16 years, they are required to apply for the first adult passport and first ID card.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK in BLOCK LETTERS only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “N/A” (not applicable).
It takes six to eight weeks to process new South African documents. To ensure that applicants receive their documents on time, they are advised to please book an appointment at least EIGHTEEN months before expiry of current passport to avoid any delays. Please do not make firm travel arrangements before obtaining your passport.
Kindly note that both parents and the child must be present at the Application Center for the child passport application.
If applicant is under 15 and a half years, they are not required to provide fingerprints.
If the child is older than 15 and a half years or turning 16 years, they are required to apply for the first adult passport and first ID card.

## Fees
Once you have made your application, you will have to pay the fee at the Application Centre by Credit/Debit card only. Kindly note that other modes of payment are not accepted.
VFS Global charges a service fee – 60 USD Euros on top of the below consular fees and additional services fees (https://services.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/zap/additional-services).

[zap > moz > en > Consular Service > Passport > Fees > table]

## Documents Required
It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document which is certified and a photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator original document (foreign birth) must be legalised/apostilled.
- Form BI-73 - Passport Application Form
Applicant to complete the section A and sign in section E of the DHA 73 form.
Section B of this form will complete upon your appointment at office.
Parental consent on page 2 section C of DHA 73 must be signed in the office if applicant is under 18 years and both parents must be present at the office for the passport of applicants below 18 years.
If one parent is not in The Netherlands, consent must be obtained from the Home Affairs in South Africa or from a local South African Embassy to complete the DHA-73 form and give consent for the child’s passport application where the other parent is residing.
In a case where parents have passed on, the lawful guardian will submit the certified copy of the court order certificate of adoption and certified copy of the first page of his/her identity document and passport.
- Form BI-529 - Determination of Citizenship Status
This form is required for all passport applications made outside of South Africa.
Please mark N/A to questions that are not applicable. Do not leave anything blank. Please note questions A9 & A10 MUST be answered and section 'E' signed by the parent of the child or the form will be returned.
- Form DI-47 Loss of passport report and police report
To be submitted for lost/stolen passport with police report - if applicable.
- Bring the original and copies of your below documents
This Application Centre can certify the copy if the original is shown.
- South Africa current passport.
- South Africa birth certificate.
- South Africa ID books / smart ID cards of both parents.
- South Africa passports of both parents.
- A certified copy of your foreign citizenship certificate and your dual citizenship certificate - if applicable
If you have dual nationality, please provide proof of how and when you acquired the foreign nationality, such Naturalization and retention or Registration Certificate.
FOREIGN PASSPORT OR ID IS NOT PROOF OF HOW AND WHEN YOU ACQUIRED FOREIGN CITIZENSHIP.
In cases of the applicant who acquired foreign citizenship through descent from a parent, a certified copy of your own full birth certificate showing the birth places of your parent(s) is required. Under circumstances where even the parents acquired such citizenship through descent, a letter from the relevant authorities of the country concerned will suffice.
If you are a naturalised South African, a certified copy of your Naturalisation Certificate.
Documents issued in a foreign language (if not in English) must be officially translated to English by a sworn translator.
This Application Centre can certify the copy as a “true copy of the original” if the original is provided.

## Photo Specifications
Four (4) clear, professionally taken colour photos of full face of applicant, showing only head and shoulders measuring 35mm x 45mm.
This Service can obtain at Application Centre with applicable fee.
Please do not attach the photos to the form. Officials at the Application Centre will certify photos on the day of your appointment.

## Processing Time
The standard processing time is approximately 6 to 8 weeks from the date of submission of the application.
Please note that, following submission, applications are subject to additional verification and screening by the relevant authorities.
Applicants may be requested to:
- Provide additional supporting documentations;
- Re-submit biometric data (e.g. where fingerprint or photograph quality does not meet required standards);
- Re-submit application forms (in instances of incomplete or inaccurate information in currently submitted forms).
Where additional requirements arise, processing times may be extended accordingly.
Applicants are strongly advised to schedule an appointment at least eighteen (18) months prior to the expiry date of their current passport in order to ensure the timely receipt of travel documents and to avoid potential delays.
Applicants are further advised not to make any confirmed or non-refundable travel arrangements until the new passport has been issued and successfully received.
Disclaimer
Please be informed that VFS Global does not have any influence over the processing timelines for passport applications, first identity document applications, or birth registration services.

## Download Forms
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “N/A” (not applicable).
- DHA - 73_Passport Application (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ChDh49d3AmEsE5y0gKvul/456c6ff0f95ed0cda72b8e5d12e12a19/dha-73.pdf)
- BI-529_Determination of Citizenship (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3MDVNjoL4DEar34u8rdR1s/86b75f81835fff7663112fe88d30bd22/BI-529-determination-of-citizenship.pdf)
- DI – 47_Loss Report (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/22y5oLag2SNxtzNnoNDpdP/0a07f2396b45c18da511cd5aeae3990d/DI-47_Loss_Report.pdf)
- Registration of divorce (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4wHrprxkynDG7cbMqo4vSQ/1a64acaeee5eb005b1900e716cfd5733/REGISTRATION_OF_DIVORCE.pdf)
- Registration of Marriage (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/56n8Be3ZSuYD4lI1wky69t/d58b2281e14bd0f21c6dba320da947fc/Registration_of_Marriage.pdf)
- Namibian Questionnaire (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1qNJWtnX7M22XrWBehlLEf/94d933661286544292727681b7a02805/NAMIBIAN_QUESTIONNAIRE.pdf)

visa_types.name: Registration of Foreign Birth
visa_types.documents_required:
Getting copies of Birth Certificates
South African citizens, whether in the Republic or outside the Republic, including persons who are not South African citizens but who sojourn permanently or temporarily in the Republic, for whatever purpose.
- DHA-24 Form - Notice of Birth
- DHA 288 Form (Affidavit in Support of Notice of Birth)
- DHA-154 form (Application for a birth certificate)
- Copy of the South Africa Passport / ID Card
- Copy of the South Africa Birth Certificate
- Copy of the parent’s ID Card / ID Book
Registration of a Birth within 30 days
A parent, parents, guardian or any other person legally responsible for the child must complete Form DHA-24 (with black ink only) and it must be submitted to the Application Centre, along with below documents.
Once the child’s birth has been registered, an unabridged birth certificate is issued free of charge
- DHA-24 Form - Notice of Birth
- DHA 288 Form (Affidavit in Support of Notice of Birth)
- BI 529 for child (Determination of Citizenship Status)
- BI 529 for both parent’s (Determination of Citizenship Status)
- Foreign birth certificate of the child
- Foreign ID documents of the child
- Birth certificate of the child’s parent’s
- Marriage certificate of the child’s parent’s
- ID and Passport of the child’s parent’s
- Parent’s foreign citizenship certificate – if applicable
- Two (2) clear, professionally taken colour photos of full face of the child, showing only head and shoulders measuring 35mm x 45mm.
Registration of a Birth above 30 days
Kindly request to contact the Embassy of South Africa in Netherlands to submit the application for registration of a birth above 30 days.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Once you have made your application, you will have to pay the fee at the DHA Services Centre by Credit/Debit card only. Kindly note that other modes of payment are not accepted.
VFS Global charges a service fee – 60 USD on top of the below consular fees and additional services fees (https://services.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/zap/additional-services).

[zap > moz > en > Consular Service > Registration of Foreign Birth > Fees > table]

The standard processing time is approximately 6 to 8 weeks from the date of submission of the application.
Please note that, following submission, applications are subject to additional verification and screening by the relevant authorities.
Applicants may be requested to:
- Provide additional supporting documentations;
- Re-submit biometric data (e.g. where fingerprint or photograph quality does not meet required standards);
- Re-submit application forms (in instances of incomplete or inaccurate information in currently submitted forms).
Where additional requirements arise, processing times may be extended accordingly.
Applicants are strongly advised to schedule an appointment at least eighteen (18) months prior to the expiry date of their current passport in order to ensure the timely receipt of travel documents and to avoid potential delays.
Applicants are further advised not to make any confirmed or non-refundable travel arrangements until the new passport has been issued and successfully received.
Disclaimer
Please be informed that VFS Global does not have any influence over the processing timelines for passport applications, first identity document applications, or birth registration services.
visa_types.forms:
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “*N/A*” (not applicable).
- DHA -24_Notice of Birth
- DHA-154_Birth Certificate
- DHA – 288_Affidavit_Notice of Birth
- BI-529-Determination-of-Citizenship
- Form SVP – 109E For citizens born in Namibia
visa_types.overview:
Overview
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK in BLOCK LETTERS only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “*N/A*” (not applicable).
It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document along with a certified photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator and original document must be legalised/apostilled.
Processing time is approximately SIX TO EIGHT WEEKS as applications are forwarded to Department of Home Affairs in Pretoria for processing.

visa_types.name: Adult Passport
visa_types.documents_required:
It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document which is certified and a photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator original document (foreign birth or marriage certificate & divorcee decree) must be legalised/apostilled.
- Form DHA-73 Passport Application Form
Applicant to complete the section A and sign in section E of the DHA 73form.
Section B of this form will complete upon your appointment at office.
Parental consent on page 2 section C of DHA 73 must be signed in the office if applicant is under 18 years and both parents must be present in the office for the passport of applicants below 18 years.
If one parent is not in the New Zealand, consent must be obtained from the Home Affairs in South Africa or from a local South African Embassy to complete the DHA-73 form and give consent for the child’s passport application where the other parent is residing.
In a case where parents have passed on, the lawful guardian will submit the certified copy of the court order certificate of adoption and certified copy of the first page of his/her identity document and passport.
- DHA-529 - Determination of Citizenship Status
This form is required for all passport applications applied for outside of South Africa. Please mark N/A to questions that are not applicable - do not leave anything blank.
Please note questions A9 & A10 MUST be answered and section 'E' signed by the applicant or the form will be returned.
- Form DHA-9 Full set of fingerprints
This form is not downloadable. You will receive it to complete on the day of your appointment at Application Centre.
- Form DI-47 Loss of passport report and police report
To be submitted for lost/stolen passport with police report - if applicable.
- Bring the original and copies of your below documents
This Application Centre can certify the copy as a “true copy of the original” if the original is shown.
- South Africa current passport.
- South Africa Identity document. (ID book / Smart ID Card)
- South Africa birth certificate.
- Marriage certificate, if married surname to be mentioned in passport.
- South Africa ID books / smart ID cards and passports of both parents, if applicant is under 18 years for first adult passport (Kindly note that both parents and the child must be present at the Application Center for the first adult passport application).
- A certified copy of your foreign citizenship certificate and your dual citizenship certificate - if applicable
If you have dual nationality, please provide proof of how and when you acquired the foreign nationality, such Naturalization and retention or Registration Certificate.
FOREIGN PASSPORT OR ID IS NOT PROOF OF HOW AND WHEN YOU ACQUIRED FOREIGN CITIZENSHIP.
In cases of the applicant who acquired foreign citizenship through descent from a parent, a certified copy of your own full birth certificate showing the birth places of your parent(s) is required. Under circumstances where even the parents acquired such citizenship through descent, a letter from the relevant authorities of the country concerned will suffice.
If you are a naturalised South African, a certified copy of your Naturalisation Certificate.
Documents issued in a foreign language must be officially translated to English by a sworn translator. This Application Centre can certify the copy as a “true copy of the original” if the original is provided.
- Applicable to women to mentioned married surname in passport
If you want your passport to be issued in your maiden / married surname, a formal letter of request must also be included together with a certified copy of your marriage certificate.
Foreign marriage certificate (if married outside SA) must submit ID/Passport of spouse together with the marriage registration form available on our website.
Foreign divorce certificate (if divorced outside SA) if possible, submit ID/Passport of spouse together with the divorced registration form available on our website.
Final Divorce Decree, both copies of marriage certificate and final divorce decree must be submitted.

Once you have made your application, you will have to pay the fee at the Application Centre by Credit/Debit card only. Kindly note that other modes of payment are not accepted.
VFS Global charges a service fee – 60 USD Euros on top of the below consular fees and additional services fees (https://services.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/zap/additional-services).

Overview
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK in BLOCK LETTERS only.
Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “N/A” (not applicable).
Applicants applying for first adult passport (i.e. applicants 15 and a half years old or turning 16) must apply for first ID card before applying for the passport or along with the first adult passport application as this is now compulsory by the Department of Home Affairs.
Kindly note that both parents and the child must be present at the Application Center for the first adult passport application.
It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document which is certified and a photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator original document (foreign birth or marriage certificate & divorcee decree) must be legalised/apostilled.
To ensure that applicants receive their documents on time, they are advised to please book an appointment at least EIGHTEEN months before expiry of current passport to avoid any delays. Please do not make firm travel arrangements before obtaining your passport.

It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document which is certified and a photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator original document (foreign birth or marriage certificate & divorcee decree) must be legalised/apostilled.
To ensure that applicants receive their documents on time, they are advised to please book an appointment at least EIGHTEEN months before expiry of current passport to avoid any delays. Please do not make firm travel arrangements before obtaining your passport.

## Fees
Once you have made your application, you will have to pay the fee at the Application Centre by Credit/Debit card only. Kindly note that other modes of payment are not accepted.
VFS Global charges a service fee – 60 USD Euros on top of the below consular fees and additional services fees (https://services.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/zap/additional-services).

[zap > moz > en > Consular Service > Passport > Fees > table]

## Documents Required
It is important that you bring the correct documents with you. For every document submitted, you must provide the original document which is certified and a photocopy. If your certified documents are not in English, the certified documents must be translated into English by a sworn translator original document (foreign birth or marriage certificate & divorcee decree) must be legalised/apostilled.
- Form DHA-73 Passport Application Form
Applicant to complete the section A and sign in section E of the DHA 73form.
Section B of this form will complete upon your appointment at office.
Parental consent on page 2 section C of DHA 73 must be signed in the office if applicant is under 18 years and both parents must be present in the office for the passport of applicants below 18 years.
If one parent is not in the New Zealand, consent must be obtained from the Home Affairs in South Africa or from a local South African Embassy to complete the DHA-73 form and give consent for the child’s passport application where the other parent is residing.
In a case where parents have passed on, the lawful guardian will submit the certified copy of the court order certificate of adoption and certified copy of the first page of his/her identity document and passport.
- DHA-529 - Determination of Citizenship Status
This form is required for all passport applications applied for outside of South Africa. Please mark N/A to questions that are not applicable - do not leave anything blank.
Please note questions A9 & A10 MUST be answered and section 'E' signed by the applicant or the form will be returned.
- Form DHA-9 Full set of fingerprints
This form is not downloadable. You will receive it to complete on the day of your appointment at Application Centre.
- Form DI-47 Loss of passport report and police report
To be submitted for lost/stolen passport with police report - if applicable.
- Bring the original and copies of your below documents
This Application Centre can certify the copy as a “true copy of the original” if the original is shown.
- South Africa current passport.
- South Africa Identity document. (ID book / Smart ID Card)
- South Africa birth certificate.
- Marriage certificate, if married surname to be mentioned in passport.
- South Africa ID books / smart ID cards and passports of both parents, if applicant is under 18 years for first adult passport (Kindly note that both parents and the child must be present at the Application Center for the first adult passport application).
- A certified copy of your foreign citizenship certificate and your dual citizenship certificate - if applicable
If you have dual nationality, please provide proof of how and when you acquired the foreign nationality, such Naturalization and retention or Registration Certificate.
FOREIGN PASSPORT OR ID IS NOT PROOF OF HOW AND WHEN YOU ACQUIRED FOREIGN CITIZENSHIP.
In cases of the applicant who acquired foreign citizenship through descent from a parent, a certified copy of your own full birth certificate showing the birth places of your parent(s) is required. Under circumstances where even the parents acquired such citizenship through descent, a letter from the relevant authorities of the country concerned will suffice.
If you are a naturalised South African, a certified copy of your Naturalisation Certificate.
Documents issued in a foreign language must be officially translated to English by a sworn translator. This Application Centre can certify the copy as a “true copy of the original” if the original is provided.
- Applicable to women to mentioned married surname in passport
If you want your passport to be issued in your maiden / married surname, a formal letter of request must also be included together with a certified copy of your marriage certificate.
Foreign marriage certificate (if married outside SA) must submit ID/Passport of spouse together with the marriage registration form available on our website.
Foreign divorce certificate (if divorced outside SA) if possible, submit ID/Passport of spouse together with the divorced registration form available on our website.
Final Divorce Decree, both copies of marriage certificate and final divorce decree must be submitted.

## Photo Specifications
Four (4) clear, professionally taken colour photos of full face of applicant, showing only head and shoulders measuring 35mm x 45mm.
This Service can obtain at Application Centre with applicable fee.
Please do not attach the photos to the form. Officials at the Application Centre will certify photos on the day of your appointment.

## Processing Time
The standard processing time is approximately 6 to 8 weeks from the date of submission of the application.
Please note that, following submission, applications are subject to additional verification and screening by the relevant authorities.
Applicants may be requested to:
- Provide additional supporting documentations;
- Re-submit biometric data (e.g. where fingerprint or photograph quality does not meet required standards);
- Re-submit application forms (in instances of incomplete or inaccurate information in currently submitted forms).
Where additional requirements arise, processing times may be extended accordingly.
Applicants are strongly advised to schedule an appointment at least eighteen (18) months prior to the expiry date of their current passport in order to ensure the timely receipt of travel documents and to avoid potential delays.
Applicants are further advised not to make any confirmed or non-refundable travel arrangements until the new passport has been issued and successfully received.
Disclaimer
Please be informed that VFS Global does not have any influence over the processing timelines for passport applications, first identity document applications, or birth registration services.

## Download Forms
All forms need to be completed in BLACK INK only. Do Not Use Tippex or White-out. The application will be rejected by DHA.
Please ensure that you completely answer the forms-leaving no blanks. If something is irrelevant, please indicate so with an “N/A” (not applicable).
- DHA - 73_Passport Application (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ChDh49d3AmEsE5y0gKvul/456c6ff0f95ed0cda72b8e5d12e12a19/dha-73.pdf)
- BI-529_Determination of Citizenship (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3MDVNjoL4DEar34u8rdR1s/86b75f81835fff7663112fe88d30bd22/BI-529-determination-of-citizenship.pdf)
- DI – 47_Loss Report (http://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/22y5oLag2SNxtzNnoNDpdP/0a07f2396b45c18da511cd5aeae3990d/DI-47_Loss_Report.pdf)
- Form SVP – 109E For citizens born in Namibia (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/southafrica/netherlands/passport-services/pdf/7-NAMIBIAN_QUESTIONNAIRE-2026.pdf)
